title: David Copperfield - Tome II
summaries: "David Copperfield - Tome II" by Charles Dickens is a novel published in 1850. This bildungsroman follows David's journey from childhood to maturity in Victorian England. After escaping his cruel stepfather and finding refuge with his eccentric aunt, David pursues education and eventually a career as a proctor and writer. Along the way, he encounters memorable characters including the devious clerk Uriah Heep, the steadfast Agnes Wickfield, and the debt-ridden Micawber family, while navigating love, loss, and his painful social ascent.
